behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local scoring logic (threshold lookup + arithmetic), not a module handoff requirement. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior text is only about assigning a score from configured thresholds.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function with in-memory config for merchant-category thresholds and a transaction just above threshold; assert exact numeric risk score and boundary behavior (equal vs greater-than).

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP (AI/ML-DEP): correctness depends on real model judgment of a pattern, not deterministic wrapper logic. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed model outputs would always pass and cannot reveal missed fraud pattern detection. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern' (output-quality requirement).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on labeled transaction sequences where rule thresholds are individually below limits but the combined pattern is fraudulent; assert fraud probability/label crosses decision threshold with acceptable detection rate.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is counting/window boundary logic per user, which can be reproduced in one local function with controlled timestamps. REAL-INFRA: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration/System disqualified because behavior does not require real datastore or scheduler semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Pass an in-memory transaction list with mixed users and edge timestamps (inside, outside, exactly at window boundary) into velocity-count logic; assert per-user counts and boundary inclusion rules.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff/orchestration boundary (combining two model outputs into hold decision). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (not System); no dependence on model output quality because flags can be fixed inputs (not Agentic); single decision step (not Workflow). Rejection-gate: Agentic disqualified by behavior text that prespecifies model outputs ('flagged by risk model but not by scoring model') and tests gate semantics only.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scorer output + risk-model output through decision service using deterministic stubs: scorer=false, risk=true; assert transaction status is HOLD_FOR_REVIEW and AND semantics do not occur.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a contract/aggregation behavior across scoring output, risk output, and combined response DTO. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (not System); correctness does not depend on whether the probability is accurate, only that it is present and mapped correctly (not Agentic); no multi-agent sequence (not Workflow). Rejection-gate: Agentic disqualified because behavior text is about output composition, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute aggregator/response layer with deterministic model outputs and assert response contains numeric score, fraud probability, and per-model flag metadata fields with correct values and field names.
